이사야 21:1.content: The burden of the wilderness of the sea. As whirlwinds in the South sweep through, it comes from the wilderness, from an awesome land.

이사야 21:2.content: A grievous vision is declared to me. The treacherous man deals treacherously, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, Elam; attack! I have stopped all of Media’s sighing.

이사야 21:3.content: Therefore my thighs are filled with anguish. Pains have seized me, like the pains of a woman in labor. I am in so much pain that I can’t hear. I so am dismayed that I can’t see.

이사야 21:4.content: My heart flutters. Horror has frightened me. The twilight that I desired has been turned into trembling for me.

이사야 21:5.content: They prepare the table. They set the watch. They eat. They drink. Rise up, you princes, oil the shield!

이사야 21:6.content: For the Lord said to me, “Go, set a watchman. Let him declare what he sees.

이사야 21:7.content: When he sees a troop, horsemen in pairs, a troop of donkeys, a troop of camels, he shall listen diligently with great attentiveness.”

이사야 21:8.content: He cried like a lion: “Lord, I stand continually on the watchtower in the daytime, and every night I stay at my post.

이사야 21:9.content: Behold, here comes a troop of men, horsemen in pairs.” He answered, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon; and all the engraved images of her gods are broken to the ground.

이사야 21:10.content: You are my threshing, and the grain of my floor!” That which I have heard from Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, I have declared to you.

이사야 21:11.content: The burden of Dumah. One calls to me out of Seir, “Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?”

이사야 21:12.content: The watchman said, “The morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire. Come back again.”

이사야 21:13.content: The burden on Arabia. In the forest in Arabia you will lodge, you caravans of Dedanites.

이사야 21:14.content: They brought water to him who was thirsty. The inhabitants of the land of Tema met the fugitives with their bread.

이사야 21:15.content: For they fled away from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the heat of battle.

이사야 21:16.content: For the Lord said to me, “Within a year, as a worker bound by contract would count it, all the glory of Kedar will fail,

이사야 21:17.content: and the residue of the number of the archers, the mighty men of the children of Kedar, will be few; for Yahweh, the God of Israel, has spoken it.”
